ingredients.address: Kagawa prefecture agricultural cooperative.Snuggle up close hall Ogi., 129-3 Ogicho, Takamatsu, Kagawa 760-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The agricultural cooperative building on Ogijima appears in the opening episode as a background structure in the village, placing Takahara in the everyday agricultural and fishing life of Torishirojima from his first day ashore.

ingredients.address: Sumiyoshi Taisha, 2 Chome-9-89 Sumiyoshi, Sumiyoshi Ward, Osaka, 558-0045, Japan

ingredients.name: Naoshima Commerce and Industry Association
ingredients.address: Naoshima Commerce and Industry Association, 2249-22 宮ノ浦 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The commerce association building in Naoshima's Miyanoura district appears in scenes depicting the island's commercial hub, visited by characters on errands that reveal the quiet tempo of island life central to the series' atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Ogi Island Lighthouse.
ingredients.address: Ogi Island Lighthouse., 1062-3 Ogicho, Takamatsu, Kagawa 760-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The white lighthouse at the tip of Ogijima appears as the iconic landmark of Torishirojima, visible from the ferry and featured in the opening moments when Harukazedoi Takahara first glimpses the island. Pilgrims climb the hill path to recreate the sweeping sea-and-sky view that defines the anime's summer aesthetic.

ingredients.name: Uno Station
ingredients.address: Uno Station, 1-chōme-1 Chikkō, Tamano, Okayama 706-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tamano's Uno Station on the Okayama side is the mainland departure point for the ferry to the islands, appearing in scenes when Takahara first arrives from the mainland and again at the end of summer when he faces leaving.

ingredients.name: Naoshima New Museum of Art
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒761-3110 Kagawa, Kagawa District, Naoshima, Naoshima New Museum of Art, ⾹川郡直島町 3299-73

ingredients.name: Naoshima Port (Honmura) Stone Lantern
ingredients.address: Naoshima Port (Honmura) Stone Lantern, 841-4 本村 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The historic stone lantern at Honmura port is a recurring landmark in scenes where characters walk along the shore in the evening, its warm glow framing emotional exchanges between Takahara and the girls.

ingredients.name: The House of Sunset and Cats
ingredients.entityContext: The wooden house overlooking the Ogijima hillside is associated with cat-watching scenes in the anime, tied to the island's famous feline residents and featured in quiet afternoon moments when Takahara wanders alone through the village lanes.

ingredients.name: Yayoda
ingredients.address: Yayoda, 842-1, Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The seafood restaurant near Honmura on Naoshima appears in scenes depicting a casual meal shared by characters, with the fresh catch of the day reflecting the maritime lifestyle of the island setting.

ingredients.name: Naoshima Public Bath I Love You
ingredients.address: Naoshima Public Bath I Love Yu, 2252-2, Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The artist Shinro Ohtake's flamboyant public bathhouse on Naoshima appears in scenes set at the island sento, where characters share a relaxed moment during a rare trip away from Torishirojima. It is one of the most visually striking real locations in the show.

ingredients.name: Set menu eating house Madoka.(B&B)

ingredients.name: Ogijima Pavilion
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒760-0091 Kagawa, Takamatsu, Ogicho, 346 男木島パビリオン
ingredients.entityContext: The community pavilion on Ogijima serves as a gathering and event space in the anime, seen during the island's summer festival sequences where Takahara and the girls participate in local celebrations.

ingredients.name: Kyu-Iwasaki-tei Gardens
ingredients.address: Kyū-Iwasaki-tei Gardens, 1 Chome-3-45 Ikenohata, Taito City, Tokyo 110-0008, Japan

ingredients.name: Gotanji Swimming Beach
ingredients.localName: 琴弾地（ごたんぢ）海水浴場
ingredients.address: Gotanji Swimming Beach, 3229 積浦 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The sandy cove on Naoshima's eastern shore is the real-world stand-in for the beach where Takahara and the island girls spend a carefree afternoon, one of the series' most celebrated feel-good sequences.

ingredients.localName: 小木港 北埠頭２号岸壁
ingredients.address: Ogi Port North Terminal Pier 2, 1951 Ogimachi, Sado, Niigata 952-0604, Japan

ingredients.address: Cin.na.mon, 2310-31 宮浦 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan

ingredients.name: Pumpkin - Kusama Yayoi
ingredients.entityContext: Yayoi Kusama's iconic red polka-dot pumpkin sculpture at Naoshima's Miyanoura port appears briefly in scenes depicting the island-art world that shapes Torishirojima's fictional geography, and it serves as a background landmark when characters arrive by ferry.

ingredients.name: Marine station "Naoshima"
ingredients.address: Marine station "Naoshima", 2249番地４０, Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Naoshima ferry terminal designed by SANAA appears as the departure point for characters traveling between islands, featured in scenes where Takahara takes a day trip and reflects on how much the summer has changed him.

ingredients.name: Benzaiten
ingredients.localName: 銭洗弁財天 宇賀福神社
ingredients.address: Zeniarai Benten Shrine, 2 Chome-25-16 Sasuke, Kamakura, Kanagawa 248-0017, Japan

ingredients.name: Toyotama-hime Shrine
ingredients.address: Toyotama-hime Shrine, 1903 Ogicho, Takamatsu, Kagawa 760-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small Shinto shrine on Ogijima is the real-world basis for the island shrine featured in multiple character routes, most prominently in scenes involving Shiroha and the island's local legends. Fans visit to recreate the stone-step approach framed by lush summer foliage.

ingredients.name: Sunset and cat house
ingredients.entityContext: An alternate angle of the Ogijima house famous for its cats and sunset views, capturing the same warm late-afternoon atmosphere that recurs throughout the anime's idle summer-afternoon scenes.

ingredients.name: Former Mitsubishi Materials Naoshima Smelter & Refinery Miyanoura Company Housing
ingredients.localName: 三菱マテリアル(株) 直島製錬所
ingredients.address: Mitsubishi Materials Corporation Naoshima Smelter & Refinery, 4049-1, Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan

ingredients.name: Ogi Fishing Port
ingredients.address: Ogi Fishing Port, 大井 Ogicho, Takamatsu, Kagawa 760-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Takahara arrives by ferry at the small harbor that stands in for Torishirojima's port, where he meets the local girls for the first time. The stone quayside and fishing boats set the tone for the entire summer story.

ingredients.name: Set menu eating house Madoka.(B&B)
ingredients.localName: 民宿&食堂 円(まどか)

ingredients.name: Ando Museum
ingredients.address: Ando Museum, 736-2 本村 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tadao Ando's concrete museum embedded in a traditional Naoshima townhouse appears in scenes where characters wander the Honmura art district, with the contrast of rough concrete and old timber reflecting the show's theme of tradition meeting change.

ingredients.name: Takamatsu Shiritsu Ogisho Junior High School
ingredients.localName: 高松市立男木小・中学校
ingredients.address: Takamatsu Shiritsu Ogisho Junior High School, 1988 Ogicho, Takamatsu, Kagawa 760-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The combined primary and middle school on Ogijima appears as the island school building in the anime, seen in scenes involving the island's few remaining children. Its distinctive roofline and seaside setting are immediately recognizable to fans.

ingredients.name: Ishii Shouten
ingredients.address: Ishii Shouten, 845-1 本村 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The general store and inn on Naoshima's Honmura district appears as a neighborhood shop visited by characters during their stay, reinforcing the lived-in texture of the island community that surrounds Takahara's summer.

ingredients.name: Yokobou Park
ingredients.address: Yokobou Park, 3765-5 宮ノ浦 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small coastal park in Naoshima's Miyanoura area provides a breezy vantage point over the Seto Inland Sea, appearing in scenes where characters sit on the seawall and talk through personal issues against a backdrop of sea and distant islands.

ingredients.name: MY LODGE Naoshima
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒761-3110 Kagawa, Kagawa District, Naoshima, 3718-56 MY LODGE Naoshima

ingredients.name: SPARKY's House
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒761-3110 Kagawa, Kagawa District, Naoshima, 宮ノ浦1915 SPARKY's House

ingredients.name: Naoshima Pavilion
ingredients.address: Naoshima Pavilion, Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The lattice steel pavilion in Naoshima's bay appears in background shots establishing the island's contemporary art character, echoing scenes where characters wander near the water during a summer evening.

ingredients.name: Naoshima Dam
ingredients.address: Naoshima Dam, 1537 宮ノ浦 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small reservoir dam inland on Naoshima provides the backdrop for a quiet scene where characters escape the heat of the village, the still water reflecting the surrounding greenery in a moment of calm before a key emotional beat.

ingredients.name: Unburnt Lantern Monument
ingredients.address: Unburnt Lantern Monument, 積浦 Naoshima, Kagawa District, Kagawa 761-3110, Japan

ingredients.name: Municipal Ogi Islanders & Tourist Interaction Hall.
ingredients.address: Municipal Ogi Islanders & Tourist Interaction Hall., 1986 Ogicho, Takamatsu, Kagawa 760-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The community hall at the base of Ogijima's village functions as the communal gathering point for island residents in the story, appearing in scenes where the main group of characters meets. The building's curved roof designed by Kazunari Sakamoto is a memorable visual.

ingredients.name: East Seto Fisheries Cooperative.Ogi branch.
ingredients.localName: 東瀬戸漁協 男木支所
ingredients.address: East Seto Fisheries Cooperative.Ogi branch., 104-6 Ogicho, Takamatsu, Kagawa 760-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The fisheries cooperative building near Ogijima's harbor appears in early-episode scenes depicting everyday island life, reinforcing Torishirojima's character as a working fishing village where Takahara's grandmother lives.

ingredients.name: Daigojukyuban Kokubun Temple
ingredients.address: Iyo Kokubun Temple, 4 Chome-1-33 Kokubu, Imabari, Ehime 799-1533, Japan
